channel.title: Politics-Uk on The Daily Thrum
channel.description: Recent content in Politics-Uk on The Daily Thrum

item.title: “Deployment Needs A Manual Step In This Repo”: Starmer’s Handover To Burnham Now Underway
item.description:
LONDON — Sir Keir Starmer’s handover to Andy Burnham is reportedly underway after the outgoing Prime Minister began transferring critical government knowledge in the form of vague comments, half-updated documents and warnings that several important national systems “need manual steps”.

According to Downing Street sources, Burnham was invited to a leadership transition session on Tuesday morning titled Handover — Final FINAL, during which Starmer walked him through the current operating model of the country using a 47-page document last updated during the May cabinet.

item.title: Sydney Man Furious Cross Redhead Has Yet To Meet The Online Right During UK Trip
item.description:
SYDNEY — Local man Nathan Norton has expressed mounting frustration that Pauline Hanson’s current tour of Britain has included meetings with Holly Valance and Tommy Ten Names but, inexplicably, no confirmed encounter with Carl Benjamin.

Norton said the trip had initially shown enormous promise.

“She met Holly Valance. Good start,” he said.

“Then Tommy Robinson, Stephen Yaxley-Lennon, whatever we’re calling him this week. Fine. We’re building momentum.”

“But where the fuck are Sargon, Mr H and Dan Tubb?”

item.title: Keir Starmer Understood To Have Attended Exit Interview
item.description:
LONDON — Sir Keir Starmer is understood to have attended an exit interview with senior Labour figures this morning after confirming his intention to leave the role of Prime Minister.

According to Downing Street sources, Starmer arrived expecting a short discussion about handover arrangements, only to be shown into a small meeting room where Rachel Reeves, Shabana Mahmood and Yvette Cooper were seated behind a table with a red folder marked Leaver Feedback — Keir.

item.title: “Britain Is A Tolerant Country,” Says Councillor Who Forgot Intolerance Created The Empire
item.description:
LONDON — A local councillor has praised Britain as a “deeply tolerant country” during a community cohesion event, apparently forgetting that the British Empire was not assembled through mutual respect, patient dialogue, and a firm commitment to celebrating difference.

The remarks were made at a council forum on inclusion, belonging, and shared values, where attendees were reminded that Britain’s greatest strength has always been its tolerance, apart from the several centuries during which its greatest strength was gunboats, conquest, railways, hospitals, common law, naval supremacy, abolishing suttee, suppressing piracy, redrawing maps, and arriving in foreign ports with the relaxed confidence of a people who had already decided they were in charge.

item.title: James O’Brien Demands Caller Name A Member Of The Liberal Media Elite Who Would Call Him Racist, While Calling Him Racist
item.description:
LONDON — LBC presenter James O’Brien has reportedly challenged a pensioner to name a single member of the so-called “liberal media elite” who would call him a bigot or racist for objecting to the transformation of his neighbourhood, shortly before spending the next nine minutes strongly implying the caller was morally defective.

The exchange occurred after 74-year-old pensioner Barry Wilkes told O’Brien he felt unable to discuss rapid demographic change, housing pressure, crime, school overcrowding and the loss of social trust in his area without being dismissed by “the media, the liberal elite and people like you” as a bigot and racist.

item.title: Protesters In Southampton Use Institutional Sympathy Language, Surprised When Police Crack Their Heads Anyway
item.description:
SOUTHAMPTON — Police have reportedly proceeded to mass arrests and beatings after protesters outside Southampton Police Station deployed familiar anti-racist slogans.

The crowd gathered outside the station chanting “racist police off our streets” and “I can’t breathe”, in what witnesses described as a bold attempt to activate the moral force field normally generated when those phrases are used by university students, NGOs, activist collectives or people standing beside professionally printed placards.

item.title: UK: Pensioner Arrested For Saying Government Should Not Give Legal Exemptions To Religious Minorities Carrying Ceremonial Weapons After Man Uses Ceremonial Energised Plasma Rifle To Level Building
item.description:
LONDON — A pensioner has been arrested on suspicion of religious hatred after saying the government should not give legal exemptions to religious minorities carrying ceremonial weapons, days after a man legally carrying a ceremonial energised plasma rifle was convicted of using one to level a building, killing 12 people and injuring 28 others.

Harold Davis, 74, reportedly made the comment outside a newsagent while watching a televised debate in which politicians, police officials, community leaders and sensitivity advisers stressed that the incident should not be used to question the broader exemption framework.
